title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. albescens
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. albescens, also known as the white Daffodil or white narcissus, is a subspecies of the common daffodil that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. albescens include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat various health conditions.
